mnemonics: ACID Rain Keeps Data Dry (ACID properties: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability)
Begin Read Commit (The main TCL commands: BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK)
keyPoints: Transactions group a set of tasks into a single execution unit, where each transaction begins with a task and ends when all tasks are successfully completed.
The TCL commands (Transaction Control Language) are COMMIT to save changes, ROLLBACK to undo them, and SAVEPOINT to set a point to which a transaction can later be rolled back.
SQL transactions follow the ACID properties to ensure reliability and data integrity in database operations.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to COMMIT a transaction, leaving it open and causing locks that block other users.
Using ROLLBACK incorrectly, which can undo more work than intended, especially if SAVEPOINTs are not used to create checkpoints.
